stage-intentionally.label: Stage One Purpose at a Time
stage-intentionally.summary: Separate two supplied community-site purposes into focused snapshots.

sketch0.title: One Project Can Hold Several Purposes

sketch1.title: Stage the First Purpose by Name

try_stage_intentionally_sketch0.title: Stage Only the Heading Purpose
try_stage_intentionally_sketch0.prompt: `community-site/index.html` contains the supplied heading change, and `styles.css` contains the supplied footer-spacing change. Type `git add index.html` and press Enter. Leave styles.css unstaged.

help.concept: Intentional staging separates unrelated work before a commit exists.
help.hint_2: styles.css should remain only in the working tree.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site' mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events and Resources

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 24px; } ' > community-site/styles.css

try_stage_intentionally_sketch0.instructions: `community-site/index.html` contains the supplied heading change, and `styles.css` contains the supplied footer-spacing change. Type `git add index.html` and press Enter. Leave styles.css unstaged.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_stage_intentionally_sketch1.title: Inspect the Proposed Heading Snapshot
try_stage_intentionally_sketch1.prompt: The heading change is staged and the footer-spacing change is not. Type `git diff --staged -- index.html` and press Enter.
try_stage_intentionally_sketch1.hint: Inspect the index, not the remaining working-tree change.

help.concept: git diff --staged shows what the next commit would record.
help.hint_1: Only index.html should appear in the staged patch.
help.hint_2: The styles.css change must stay outside the proposed snapshot.

try_stage_intentionally_sketch1.instructions: The heading change is staged and the footer-spacing change is not. Type `git diff --staged -- index.html` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.title: Commit the Heading Separately
try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.prompt: The index.html heading change is staged; styles.css remains unstaged. Type `git commit -m "Update community heading"` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.
try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.hint: Commit only the staged page purpose with the exact message.

help.concept: A focused commit can leave unrelated working-tree changes for a later snapshot.
help.hint_2: After the commit, styles.css should still appear modified.

try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Update community heading"
git status --short

try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.instructions: The index.html heading change is staged; styles.css remains unstaged. Type `git commit -m "Update community heading"` and press Enter. Then type `git status --short` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you run `git commit -m "Update community heading"`?

try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.title: Record the Footer Spacing Purpose
try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.prompt: Only the supplied styles.css change remains. Type `git add styles.css` and press Enter, type `git diff --staged -- styles.css` and press Enter, then type `git commit -m "Space community footer"` and press Enter.
try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.hint: Stage, review, and commit the remaining purpose.

help.concept: The second focused commit completes the unrelated style change without mixing it into the heading commit.
help.hint_1: Review the staged patch before recording it.
help.hint_2: The final message is Space community footer.

try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.solutionCode:
git add styles.css
git diff --staged -- styles.css
git commit -m "Space community footer"

try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.instructions: Only the supplied styles.css change remains. Type `git add styles.css` and press Enter, type `git diff --staged -- styles.css` and press Enter, then type `git commit -m "Space community footer"` and press Enter.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

2.message: Did you run `git commit -m "Space community footer"`?

single-choice-1.title: Choose a Focused Snapshot
single-choice-1.prompt: Two unrelated files are modified. What best keeps the next commit focused?
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Build the next snapshot one purpose at a time, review the staged version, and reverse a staging decision without losing work.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To prepare selected changes for a commit
options.d: To list the recorded commit history

multi-choice-1.title: A Coherent Snapshot
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which practices help keep a commit focused? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-1.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Build the next snapshot one purpose at a time, review the staged version, and reverse a staging decision without losing work.
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

options.a: Stage files that serve the same project result.
options.b: Review the staged snapshot before committing.
options.c: Stage every changed file so status becomes quiet.
options.d: Use a subject that describes the result.

multi-choice-2.title: Inspect the Staged Boundary
multi-choice-2.prompt: Which commands can help verify what is staged?
multi-choice-2.hint: Choose the one answer that describes this command or concept precisely.

help.concept: Build the next snapshot one purpose at a time, review the staged version, and reverse a staging decision without losing work.
help.hint_1: Remove choices that act on a different repository layer or promise behavior Git does not provide.
help.hint_2: Prefer the exact definition used in this chapter over a nearby Git idea.

fill-blank-1.title: Write a Focused Commit Subject
fill-blank-1.prompt: The staged change expands the community events heading. Complete the subject: `git commit -m "[blank1]"`.
fill-blank-1.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Build the next snapshot one purpose at a time, review the staged version, and reverse a staging decision without losing work.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

stage-intentionally-sketch0.title: One Project Can Hold Several Purposes
stage-intentionally-sketch0.bodyMarkdown: The community-site heading and footer spacing are unrelated changes. They can exist together in the working tree without belonging in the same commit.

stage-intentionally-sketch1.title: Stage the First Purpose by Name
stage-intentionally-sketch1.bodyMarkdown: Naming `index.html` with `git add` proposes only the heading change. The stylesheet remains outside the snapshot.

stage-intentionally-sketch2.title: Review the Staged Snapshot
stage-intentionally-sketch2.bodyMarkdown: `git diff --staged -- path` shows what the next commit will record. It answers a different question from ordinary `git diff`.

stage-intentionally-sketch3.title: Commit, Then Continue
stage-intentionally-sketch3.bodyMarkdown: After the heading commit, the stylesheet remains modified. Stage and commit it separately so each history entry explains one project purpose.
